title: Artocarpus inconstantissimus
taxonomy: Artocarpus inconstantissimus is a species within the genus Artocarpus, which belongs to the family Moraceae, commonly known as the mulberry or fig family. This classification reflects the plant's characteristics, such as its fruit structure and leaf morphology, which are shared with other members of the Moraceae family. The Artocarpus genus, to which Artocarpus inconstantissimus belongs, is native to the Pacific Islands and is known for its diverse species. The Moraceae family consists of approximately 600 species, including figs, mulberries, and breadfruits. Artocarpus inconstantissimus is one of the lesser-known species within this diverse family.
uses: Artocarpus inconstantissimus is a lesser-known species within the Artocarpus genus, and research on its specific medicinal uses is limited. intro:

The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to protect against oxidative stress, inflammation, and infections.
